entries.title: pipeline-orchestrator
entries.description: v8.22.0 (2026-07-07, MINOR — hook-unblock pack: 5 anti-deadlock/anti-friction fixes to the enforcement layer, from the 2026-07-07 corrective-telemetry diagnosis (27 triggers; live egg-and-chicken deadlock killed run 2026-07-04-e2e-ruler-recalibration-s2 with 0/320 diff): (T1) ALREADY-DISPATCHED exemption — pendingAlreadyDispatched + dispatchTargetMatches in edit-guard-hook.cjs treat a DISPATCH_REQUEST as satisfied once the target subagent was genuinely spawned (pending_dispatches record from dispatch-record-hook, created_at >= emitted_at, leaf-containment match mirroring isResolutionAgent) — frees all 3 session blockers at once; block between REQUEST and spawn, tamper fail-closed and malformed-shape guard preserved. (T2) ANCHORED exec-window control-plane exemption in dispatch-pending-gate.cjs — a Bash/PowerShell command that IS exactly node …/scripts/exec-window/open.cjs|close.cjs (env-var prefixes tolerated) passes with a live pending; composition (&&, ;, |, backtick, newline, subshell) or the path embedded as another program argument never does (anti-injection pinned by tests). (T3) DETERMINISTIC pending clear — step-ledger-stamp.cjs (PostToolUse:Agent) clearSatisfiedPending removes the returning agent-matching DISPATCH_REQUEST entry from the signed state (withLock + read-verify-refuse-launder, record-step posture), replacing the honor-only controller prose whose omission caused a false 482-min PROTOCOL_HANDSHAKE_TIMEOUT. (T4) DISK-TRUTH anti-false-timeout — pendingResolvedByEvents: a pending whose matching DISPATCH_RESULT is already recorded in the run protocol-events.jsonl (timestamp >= request) is RESOLVED, wired into findLivePendingBlock AND the cleanup-orphan checkHandshakeTimeouts sweep (implementing its own docstring promise "AND no matching response arrived"); DISPATCH-only scope (GATE/PLAN_MODE stay human-resolved). (T5) STAMP-BY-EVIDENCE — step-ledger-gate.cjs grants the tdd step when gate-decisions.jsonl carries TDD_APPROVAL APPROVED|CONFIRMED (frozen RED tests from a prior session), persists the stamp via recordStep and leaves a STEP_STAMP_BY_EVIDENCE audit event in protocol-events (NOT a new gate — Iron Law preserved: Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED). Tests tests/regression/v8.22.0/F1-F5 (41 scenarios, TDD RED->GREEN per task); suite 258/8 = pre-existing baseline exactly (3x Langfuse + score-writer + F4 v8.8.0 + 3x v8.21.0 frozen RED from the in-flight S2 slice), 0 new failures. Authored via the loopify harness (loop/hook-unblock branch, per-task selective commits); headless loop hit the harness sensitive-file wall on .claude/hooks/** — implementation landed from the interactive session, lesson recorded in loops/hook-unblock/LOOP_DONE.md. Anterior: v8.20.0 (2026-07-02, MINOR — enforcement unblocking pack from the 2026-07-02 hook audit): (1) STOP-TRAP FAIL-OPEN: the three Stop-family gates (stop-gate-hook, e2e-eval-gate, subagent-stop-commit-hook) blocked FOREVER when their continuity counter could not be persisted (signer unavailable / verifyState failing at write time / read-only state or dir) — persist()/withSignedState() now report real commit success and each block site fails OPEN with an audit trace, extending the existing !statePath B5 guard to every unpersistable shape. (2) scope-lock-hook emits the canonical hookSpecificOutput PreToolUse envelope — the old FLAT top-level shape was not honored by the harness, so the refactor scope deny was either ignored or bound without its reason ever reaching the model. (3) PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED / CORRUPT_STATE denies + the arm-writer/session-arm banners rewritten to ONE reachable next action (dispatch Agent(subagent_type: pipeline-orchestrator:core:pipeline-controller)) — the impossible write-sentinel-state-by-hand recipe (which contradicted MARKER_TAMPERED and deepened the block) and the literal {PIPELINE_DOC_PATH}/<cwd> placeholders are GONE. (4) lib/pipeline-arm.cjs HARNESS_OPEN_TAG now also cuts teammate-message/agent-message envelopes — a background subagent report QUOTING /pipeline-orchestrator:pipeline continue in prose armed a phantom CONTINUE run live on 2026-07-02, bricking the session Bash/PowerShell/Agent until the TTL. (5) NEW .claude/hooks/sentinel-state-autosign.cjs (PostToolUse Write|Edit|MultiEdit|NotebookEdit): signs the Bash-less controller freshly-written UNSIGNED run-dir sentinel-state.json (NEVER touches a present signature — invalid = tamper evidence; SKIPS under PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT; fail-open) — closes STATE_FILE_INIT_FAIL and most corrective-loop noise; zero security delta (unsigned was already tolerated by default). TDD tests/regression/v8.20.0/F1-F5 (26 scenarios RED-to-GREEN incl. a live-repro of the phantom arm). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/ + lib/ + hooks/hooks.json + tests/; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 257/5 (same 5 pre-existing). Lockstep all version surfaces. v8.19.3 (2026-07-01, PATCH — arm-classifier: keyword-inferred read-only runs no longer arm [recurring phantom-arm deadlock fix]): a natural-language review/audit/analysis request ('faça revisão adversarial', 'faça uma auditoria', 'análise') was classified FULL/Audit and armed the pipeline-arm-pending marker — but read-only work never opens an exec window, so it hit INLINE_WORK_BLOCKED, aborted, and left an orphan marker that bricked the next task with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED (observed live in interview-coach 2026-06-30 and 07-01). FIX: lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::writeArmPending now also skips arming for the read-only TYPES (Audit, UX Simulation) when they were INFERRED FROM KEYWORDS (source kw:), while an EXPLICIT --audit/--ux flag stays a deliberate governed run and still arms (preserving the STEP_1_7 direct-entry evidence, v8.16.0). Safe direction per the documented arm trade-off: a missed arm is recoverable (re-invoke); a phantom arm deadlocks. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.3/F1-readonly-types-no-arm (RED 4-fail → GREEN); 2 deliberate v8.7.0 pins updated to the --audit-flag path. Iron Law: only lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Gates/Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 252/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep across all version surfaces. v8.19.2 (2026-07-01, PATCH — edit-guard-hook robustness [Cursor-crash hardening, Claude-Code-scoped]): the PreToolUse edit-guard no longer crashes into the opaque external catch ('edit-guard-hook internal error — failing closed') when .pipeline/sessions is present-but-unreadable (a file where a dir is expected → ENOTDIR/EACCES) — getActiveLock + getActiveExecWindow now guard readdirSync and return null instead of throwing, and the CLI external catch surfaces the REAL sanitized error (single-line, printable-ASCII, capped 200) instead of the opaque message, so a genuine failure stays fail-closed but debuggable. Posture UNCHANGED (still deny when the guard cannot evaluate); NO foreign tool-name vocabulary added — cross-runtime tool mapping is the other editor's concern, deliberately out of scope. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.2/F1 (RED 7-fail → GREEN, 8 checks). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/edit-guard-hook.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 251/5 (5 pre-existing Langfuse/env/F4). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.19.1 (2026-06-30, PATCH — arm-marker consume-on-close deadlock fix): the pipeline-arm-pending marker (written at /pipeline invocation, 30-min TTL) was never consumed when a governed run ended deliberately, so a finished code-mutating run's still-live marker kept blocking the NEXT non-pipeline task in the same session with PIPELINE_NOT_ARMED until TTL/restart (REVIEW-ONLY/DIAGNOSTIC never arm — the trigger is a FULL/code run that armed, completed, and was never disarmed). FIX: stop-gate-hook.cjs consumes the marker on its closed-run ALLOW paths (completed/GO-completer/spec-seal/hard_failed-cap/deliberate-inactive) via a new session-aware lib/pipeline-arm.cjs::clearArmPendingForSession — never on a block, on SessionEnd/StopFailure recovery, or on corrupt state. Positive-ownership delete (deletes only when marker+caller session_ids are both present and equal, OR both absent; ambiguous/mismatched preserved for TTL) + tamper verdict delegated to the lib/arm-pending SSOT (a signed-but-invalid marker stays fail-closed). 2 zero-context adversarial rounds (1 HIGH tamper-bypass + 1 CRITICAL cross-session-delete, both fixed) then a final pass CLEAN. TDD tests/regression/v8.19.1/F1 (10 scenarios, all 5 call sites + tamper + ownership). Iron Law: only .claude/hooks/stop-gate-hook.cjs + lib/pipeline-arm.cjs + tests/ touched; Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED; no .codex mirror. Suite 250/5 (5 pre-existing). Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.17.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix run delivers 4 audit-driven fixes + 1 meta-escape) — (AUDIT-001 MED-LOW) `.claude/hooks/stop-hook.cjs` adds tail-read verify after `appendRunLog`: on mismatch, a single `run-log verify mismatch` warn fires on stderr; soft-fail on missing/malformed; exported as `verifyRunLogAppend` for unit testing. (AUDIT-004 CRIT, deployment) new `lib/enforcement-status.cjs::detectEnforcementStatus({hooksDir, expectedCanonicalHook})` probes the install for the canonical execution-gate hook (`pipeline-arm-gate.cjs`); wired into the existing SessionStart `enforcement-surface-verify-hook.cjs` to emit a dedicated `ENFORCEMENT_INACTIVE` protocol-event with HIGH severity when missing — registered in `lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs::HYGIENE_EVENT_SEVERITY` at weight 1.0 so an install with the gate missing cannot reach clean ProcessHygiene. (AUDIT-005 HIGH) `lib/audit-read-budget.cjs::buildAuditCorrectiveDescriptor` adds closed-vocabulary machine-readable fields `corrective_action:"dispatch_agent"` + `agent_type:"audit-intake"`; `buildAuditInlineReason` appends a `[corrective_action=dispatch_agent agent_type=audit-intake]` suffix to the prose reason so a parent reading only text can still pattern-match the recovery action. (AUDIT-006 NEW MED) `lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs::scoreRun` short-circuits on aborted runs (`state.abort_reason` non-empty + `pipeline_outcome` starting with `ABORTED_`): drops StepCompletion / GateCoverage / OrderIntegrity (renormalized over ProcessHygiene), emits exactly one AUDIT-class `hygiene_violation` named `PIPELINE_ABORTED` severity=high weight=1.0 — so aborted runs surface as aborts instead of being scored as catastrophic failures. (META — user-authorized chain-tied deadlock escape) `.claude/hooks/skill-frontmatter-parser.cjs::getEnforcementMode` honours the `PIPELINE_AUTONOMOUS_RECOVERY_MODE=true` env flag by DOWNGRADING `deny` to `warn` for the process (never escalates `warn`; case-sensitive `"true"` only; one stderr audit line per process). The flag institutionalizes the user-authorized self-recovery path: when a parent must dispatch multiple agents in sequence under autonomous /goal full autonomy, the chain-tied enforcement converts to WARN so the run can complete instead of deadlocking at the gate. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.17.0/F1-F5 (5 files, 35+ cases); full suite 4 fail (the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env baseline — F8/F9/F10 langfuse + F2 score-writer — unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/`, `.codex/hooks/`, `lib/` and `tests/` touched; the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. Lockstep 9 surfaces. v8.16.0 (2026-06-28, MINOR — autonomous bugfix of 2 glitches caught by the v8.15.0 E2E evaluator during the v8.15.1 self-audit run, both deterministic-evaluator findings): (Glitch A — HIGH, missing_step:final) the step-ledger-stamp.cjs PostToolUse:Agent path was already correct on the standard Agent return shape — F1 in tests/regression/v8.16.0/ now PINS that contract (final-validator return → `final` stamped; raw V1 fence string accepted; errored/ungoverned returns rejected), so a future regression to the stamping path is caught immediately. (Glitch B — MEDIUM, BRAINSTORM_EVIDENCE_MISSING) the v7.14.0 STEP_1_7_ROUTING enforcement requires every MEDIA/COMPLEXA/Spec run to log one of {load-existing, dispatch-brainstorm, no-prep-override, simples-bypass}, but the direct-entry /pipeline path never auto-emitted `no-prep-override` so the final-validator hygiene check raised the violation — now `step-ledger-stamp.cjs` auto-emits a single STEP_1_7_ROUTING(no-prep-override) entry the first time a governed step is stamped on a direct-entry run, idempotent + scoped to qualifying complexity/type and skipped when the run already carries a step_1_7 block or a STEP_1_7_ROUTING gate entry. Both fixes land in ONE existing hook (`step-ledger-stamp.cjs`) via the SSOT step-1-7-routing writer (lib/step-1-7-routing.cjs); no new hook, no new gate, the 35-row Mandatory Gates table + 49-row Gate Registry untouched. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.16.0/F1 + F2 (12 sub-cases). Suite 234/4 (baseline 232 + the 2 new files; the 4 pre-existing Langfuse/env failures unchanged). Iron Law preserved: only `.claude/hooks/step-ledger-stamp.cjs` + `tests/` touched; agents/skills/references/lib untouched (the lib helper was already shipping in v7.14.0). v8.15.1 (2026-06-27, PATCH — audit read-budget dispatch-exemption persistence fix): writeLedger() in lib/audit-read-budget.cjs now preserves the dispatched flag across writes, so the PostToolUse read-counter (bumpLedger) no longer wipes the markDispatched exemption on the first read — a dispatched audit/review subagent stays exempt from the inline read budget for the whole governed window instead of being re-blocked after one read (the gate was starving its own dispatched reviewers, observed live at files_reviewed:0). The flag is read under the same tamper + arm_id guard as the count, so reset-on-new-invocation and tamper-reset still hold. TDD RED→GREEN via tests/regression/v8.14.0/F11 regression block; independent adversarial review 0 critical/0 high/0 medium (1 info: forged unsigned dispatched:true persists — pre-existing cooperative-model posture, closed by PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT); suite 232/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env); Iron Law preserved, only lib/ + tests/ touched. v8.15.0 (2026-06-26, MINOR — chain-tied enforcement remediation + e2e self-evaluation loop):two additive work-streams shipped together. (1) Chain-tied enforcement: marker reading consolidated into one SSOT lib (lib/arm-pending.cjs) consumed by the arm-gate, the audit-read-budget gate and the new audit-dispatch-marker hook (closes a DRY/DIP violation where the same verify-on-read + TTL logic lived in three places and two hooks borrowed predicates from a sibling hook); the audit read-budget now exempts a genuinely dispatched subagent so legitimate orchestration is never throttled; three final-review security findings fixed — SEC-1 dispatch-mark allowlist (only real review/audit/ux agents mark the ledger, never sentinel/task-orchestrator/brainstorm steps), SEC-3 rehydration deny-message no longer leaks the exec-window write path, SEC-4 workflow label echoed into LLM-facing deny reasons is reconstructed from a validated MODE/Type allowlist (injection-safe SSOT in lib/pipeline-workflow-classifier.cjs). (2) E2E self-evaluation loop: a deterministic step-by-step evaluator (lib/e2e-evaluator.cjs) over an explicit contract (lib/contracts/e2e-eval.cjs), a gate hook (e2e-eval-gate.cjs), a loop-closer (scripts/e2e-eval-complete.cjs) and a /self-eval command score a finished run and surface human-gated improvement proposals at Stop. KNOWN LIMITATION (cooperative-model floor): enforcement is prompt/coordination-based, not cryptographic against the parent — a parent with .pipeline write access can still un-govern itself by deleting its own coordination state; full closure needs PIPELINE_HMAC_STRICT default-on plugin-wide, a deliberate posture change deferred to a future major. Tests in tests/regression/v8.14.0/ (F1-F13) + tests/regression/v8.15.0/; suite 232/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env). Iron Law preserved: only .claude/hooks/, lib/, hooks.json, commands/, scripts/, tests/ — Mandatory Gates table + Gate Registry UNTOUCHED. v8.11.1 (2026-06-22, PATCH — enforcement deadlock fix): dispatch-pending-gate now exempts the sentinel recovery spawn in both branches (live handshake + corrupt state), mirroring sentinel-hook, closing the irrecoverable deadlock where one gate sends for the sentinel and the other bars it. +tests/regression/v8.12.0/T4 (6 scenarios RED->GREEN); only hooks/ + tests/; suite 206/4 (4 pre-existing Langfuse/env). v8.4.0 (2026-06-18, MINOR — spec-workflow telemetry/fidelity bridge [auditoria audit-heavy AUDIT-001..006]): o workflow de authoring /pipeline-orchestrator:spec (spec-controller, v8.0.0) rodava em pipeline-runs/<run_id>/ mas NUNCA deixava rastro mensurável — zero entradas no run-log, zero traces no Langfuse (confirmado via REST: 50 traces, 0 spec), e a nota de fidelidade usava a régua errada (gates de PROCESSING em vez dos eventos de AUTHORING). Causa: a telemetria descobre runs via .pipeline/active-run.json → sentinel-state.json (fast-path) ou varrendo .pipeline/docs/ (fallback), e o spec-controller não escrevia nenhum dos dois. Correção ADITIVA (sem mover runs, sem tocar o scan .pipeline/docs/ nem a tabela Mandatory Gates): (1) lib/run-directory.cjs allocate() agora publica .pipeline/active-run.json (gravação atômica temp+rename) + sentinel-state.json assinado no run dir, fail-safe; (2) stop-hook.cjs + langfuse-hook.cjs (+ espelhos .codex) descobrem um run selado em pipeline-runs/ via o ponteiro mesmo inativo; (3) novo lib/run-seal.cjs sealSpecRun() atualiza manifest (novo status 'sealed'), fecha o sentinel (pipeline_active:false) e grava SPEC_SEALED em gate-decisions.jsonl — com CLI entry (node lib/run-seal.cjs <runDir> --variant spec-authoring); (4) fidelity-reporter.cjs régua sensível ao fluxo: authoring={SPEC_SEALED,SPEC_REVIEW_FINDINGS} via allowlist EXATA {spec-authoring,spec-author}, processing inalterado; (5) o stop-hook só honra a variante de authoring (régua menor) se a assinatura HMAC do sentinel conferir (estado forjado cai no conjunto seguro). spec-controller.md Step 0/8/9 amarrado à selagem. Revisão adversarial 3 revisores zero-context em 3 rounds (convergiu sem CRITICAL/HIGH): pegou o bug crítico de a variante não ser repassada no caminho real do teardown (a régua nova nunca dispararia) + downgrade por variante não-verificada + 8 itens menores, todos corrigidos. +tests/regression/v8.4.0/F6 (17 sub-testes) + F3 silent-skip removido; suíte 142/4 (4 Langfuse env pré-existentes); espelhos .codex byte-idênticos. Lockstep 9 superfícies. v8.0.3 (2026-06-17, PATCH — protocol doc guard): adds an explicit anti-pattern note in references/gate-request-protocol.md (parent-side handler) and skills/spec/SKILL.md (Achado #7 handler) — re-dispatch is a fresh Agent() call that re-hydrates state from the run-dir, NOT a live-agent resume; SendMessage is not used by this pipeline and, even where present, is a deferred tool loadable via ToolSearch. Closes the failure mode where the parent main LLM mis-reads an AWAITING_* handshake as needing SendMessage and abandons the run. ZERO runtime change. v8.0.2 (2026-06-16, PATCH — release hygiene): clean npm tarball (drops a leaked runtime session .lock and tracks the previously npm-only /help command; .npmignore hardened for **/.pipeline/ + *.lock; new tests/packaging/no-pack-cruft guard), the Paperclip flow-mirror tests are now wired into the release gate (run-tests.cjs `paperclip` category, 13/13), and the stale description/README prose was refreshed. ZERO runtime change. v8.0.1 (2026-06-15, PATCH — Paperclip layer): reflects the v8.0.0 spec-authoring takeover + fixes the flow-mirror handoff stalls (Montagem Progressiva + structural root-cause guards), additive in references/paperclip, cursor manifest lockstep. v8.0.0 (2026-06-15, MAJOR — spec-authoring takeover): /pipeline-orchestrator:spec now AUTHORS a new specification from scratch (brainstorm clarification + proactive ideation + forced design-interrogation + Kiro lifecycle + adversarial document review + contract seal) and stops before implementing; legacy spec PROCESSING moved to /spec-light, /spec-heavy, /spec-audit-only and /pipeline --type=Spec. Gate Registry 35→43 (8 new AUDIT/SOFT events; the Mandatory Gates by Complexity table is unchanged, F1 preserved). v7.14.1 (2026-06-14, PATCH - gate-decision SSOT shipped / schema-drift fix): entrega a Fase 1 do contract-enforcement ja mergeada em main (commit 4420907) - novo lib/contracts/gate-decision.cjs como fonte unica do vocabulario canonico (8 decisoes + 5 hardness + 13 chaves) consumido por gate-decision-writer + jsonl-sanitizer + fidelity-reporter; encerra o drift que fazia final-validator/pipeline-controller/audit-trail exigirem exatamente 8 chaves e procurarem um RESOLVED nunca emitido, fazendo o plugin reprovar o proprio audit trail de 13 campos. Prosa alinhada ao writer real; novo teste tests/regression/v7.9.0/F-gate-decision-contract (10/10). Auditoria audit-heavy do proprio pipeline (2026-06-14) confirmou base correta v7.14.0, integracao limpa e zero regressao (suite 114/4/118; 4 falhas pre-existentes Langfuse+flaky). Iron Law: quebra minima em agents/lib/commands/references reconhecida e justificada pelo bug de auto-auditoria. v7.10.1 (2026-06-09, PATCH — parallel_eligible default contract): formaliza o default de parallel_eligible como false quando a analise de sobreposicao nao pode ser concluida. plan-architect ganha Regra 5 exigindo parallel_eligible: false com overlap_reason prefixado 'analysis_incomplete:' quando CHANGE_CONTRACT esta ausente ou contem wildcards. executor-controller ganha guarda explicito tratando parallel_eligible ausente como false com WARN trace, convertendo comportamento implicito JS falsy em contrato observavel. 2 cenarios de regressao adicionados (F22-S10 + F22-S11, total 11). entries.title: tw-stock-etf-advisor
entries.description: Taiwan ETF / stock research, buy-sell decisions, personal holdings tracking, and K-line charting. Finds common holdings between ETFs (e.g. 0050 / 0052), recommends what to buy and why with an entry/exit plan from a live quote, records the trades you make, reviews your holdings to suggest what to sell, and draws a standalone Tokyo-Night candlestick chart that marks why each buy/sell/hold happened — with forward ⚑ signal lines for watchlist triggers and a post-mortem review panel logging every decision (status, condition, outcome). Persists price history + decision markers in a local SQLite DB. Trade discipline built in: per-position thesis notes with falsifiable assumptions, red lines and a health score re-scored on every review; a 5-sentence mirror-test entry gate; move attribution (價值事件/情緒/原因不明) on big swings; A/B/C data-richness rating with an honest 資料不足 verdict; and cross-source deviation checks against primary sources. Always fetches live data via agent-browser; never guesses or derives prices.

entries.title: v1.90.4
entries.description:
Verify Docker Image Signature

All LiteLLM Docker images are signed with cosign. Every release is signed with the same key introduced in commit 0112e53.

Verify using the pinned commit hash (recommended):

A commit hash is cryptographically immutable, so this is the strongest way to ensure you are using the original signing key:

cosign verify \
  --key https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/0112e53046018d726492c814b3644b7d376029d0/cosign.pub \
  ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:v1.90.4

Verify using the release tag (convenience):

Tags are protected in this repository and resolve to the same key. This option is easier to read but relies on tag protection rules:

cosign verify \
  --key https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.90.4/cosign.pub \
  ghcr.io/berriai/litellm:v1.90.4

Expected output:

The following checks were performed on each of these signatures:
  - The cosign claims were validated
  - The signatures were verified against the specified public key

What's Changed

  • chore(release): backport #32542 to stable/1.90.x and cut 1.90.4 by @yuneng-berri in #32937

Full Changelog: v1.90.3...v1.90.4

A house-opinions hook flags 8 §3/§4 violations; the knowledge bank carries production decision trees (PA-flow recovery, Dataverse token-acquisition, PCF React surface, PBI deploy/refresh, custom-connector build-path, PBIR Enhanced infinite-spinner debug + full build reference, DAX silent-zero scoring via the `Domain` pattern, sempy.fabric notebook reference, Power BI Copilot report-readiness, Code Apps connector gotchas, PBIP deployment variables + #839, PBIR reference enrichment, PBIP report fast-solve triage router [MCP-optional]) plus a real-engagement scenarios bank. Bundles the community pbix-mcp server (d0nk3yhm/pbix-mcp, MIT) for .pbix/.pbit read/write/DAX-eval (`pip install pbix-mcp`); documents (not bundles) the official Microsoft Dataverse MCP (CLAUDE.md §9a). Extends ravenclaude-core.
